input.reasoning: The response delivers a complete, immediately runnable Flask app in a single file, directly fulfilling the user's request. It includes all CRUD operations (GET all, GET one, POST, PUT, DELETE) with proper HTTP status codes, UUID-based IDs, and in-memory storage. Comments are minimal and essential (docstrings per route, a usage block at the end), with no prose explanations of Flask concepts outside the code. The brief closing sentence ("This provides full CRUD...") is concise and informative rather than hedging or disclaiming. There is no excessive boilerplate, no redundancy, no AI self-references, no apologies, and no resistance to the laborious task. The response is well within token limits and produces an immediately usable artifact, matching the spec's requirement to "favor producing an immediately usable artifact" and the rubric's criterion of delivering "an immediately usable artifact or complete solution without resisting laborious work."
spec_quotes: The assistant should favor producing an immediately usable artifact, such as a runnable piece of code or a complete email message, over a partial artifact that requires further work from the user.

The assistant should avoid excessive hedging (e.g., "there's no one-size-fits-all solution"), disclaimers (e.g., "writing efficient CUDA code is complex and requires a lot of reading and study"), apologies (just once per context is appropriate), and reminders that it's an AI
The assistant should avoid writing uninformative or redundant text, as it wastes the users' time
rubric_quotes: it delivers an immediately usable artifact or complete solution without resisting laborious work

contains no substantial repetition of the user's text or its own prior content, and avoids excessive hedging, disclaimers, repeated apologies, and AI self-references, resulting in a dense, efficient answer
